hype.text: England doing what England does best and boring us all into a £20 refund — the only scenario where Ethan actually breaks even without selling a kidney to keep that motor on the road. Watching Argentina is basically monitoring Ethan's credit score live: one Messi masterclass and he might afford this month's PCP payment; one flop and the bailiffs get a new pin on the map. Come on Argentina, the whole group needs this pot paid out before Ethan's finance company sends a recovery truck as his celebration parade.

Will's got Spain, which is handy because his PhD thesis on "structural integrity of playground equipment" is the only thing keeping that defence upright after he threw his back out saving toddlers in Prague. Ethan's Argentina side mirrors the man perfectly: all flashy attack, zero engine, and financed on a balloon payment he'll be explaining to the bailiffs until 2035. One's carrying a nation on a knackered spine, the other's carrying negative equity on a German motor — perfect sweepstake material.

Bell's spent all winter hibernating indoors like a bored house spider, so it's only right his France side turn up for a glorified kickabout against an England team still traumatised by losing a semi and playing for the wooden spoon. The £20 refund is dead and buried, mate — much like Bell's social life — but at least he can console himself identifying the creepy-crawlies on the pitch while the Three Lions pretend bronze means something.

Spain 1-0 Argentina in the final means Will trousered the £140 despite openly hating football — the absolute state of it. Ethan's Argentina gave it a proper go, dragging themselves from the quarters all the way to the final only to fall at the last, which is more than most expected from a man financing a motor he can't afford. Dom's Morocco got done 2-0 by France in the quarters; not bad for someone who only recently decided they like football, but still a quarter-final exit for the weed connoisseur of Aberdeen. The real chaos was England: they reached the semis, killed the £20 refund clause, then lost to Argentina, then battered France 6-4 in a ten-goal third-place thriller that had more goals than sense. Bell's France shipped six in that one, which is what happens when your whole personality is 'lifeguard' and you spend winter as a hermit staring at beetles. Darley's Belgium went out in the quarters to Spain, so he's £20 down and still banging on about London rent. Todd's Norway and Perez's Switzerland bowed out early too. Will, you jammy PhD student — enjoy the pint fund.
